values.value: Posts messages to a Microsoft Teams channel via a Power Automate workflow that receives the payload and forwards to Teams. Teams doesn't ship a first-party incoming webhook anymore (Office 365 Connectors are deprecated as of 2025), so the setup routes through a Workflow that admins build once per channel.

## Microsoft Teams setup
Microsoft deprecated Office 365 Connectors in 2025. New Teams integrations go through **Power Automate Workflows**. The workflow gives you a POST-able URL that this notifier calls with the notification payload.
### One-time setup per channel
1. In Teams, open the target channel -> channel name dropdown -> **Workflows** 2. Search for the template **"Post to a channel when a webhook request is received"** 3. Follow the template wizard - pick the team, pick the channel, sign in as needed 4. Copy the **HTTP POST URL** the wizard hands you at the end. This is the webhook.
### Salesforce setup
- **Webhook mode**: paste the workflow's HTTP POST URL into this service's `Webhook URL` field - **Named Credential mode**: create a Named Credential with the base URL of the workflow endpoint and reference it here. Rarely needed for Teams - the workflow URL already carries a signed token.
### Payload format
The notifier sends a JSON payload matching the "Post to a channel when a webhook request is received" template's expected shape. If you customized the workflow beyond the template default, the payload may not render correctly - restore the template defaults or update the workflow to parse the custom fields the notifier sends.
